description: Ablation study: impact of each retrieval/reranking component

configurations.name: bm25_only
configurations.notes: Baseline. Good for keyword-heavy queries, fails on semantic similarity.

configurations.name: hnsw_dense_only
configurations.description: Dense HNSW retrieval via Rust core, no reranking
configurations.notes: Better recall than BM25 but MRR is mediocre without reranking to fix ordering.

configurations.name: hybrid_rrf
configurations.description: BM25 + HNSW with reciprocal rank fusion (k=60)
configurations.notes: RRF gives a nice recall boost for almost zero latency cost. k=60 is from the original Cormack paper, tested k in [20,100] and 60 is still optimal.

configurations.name: hybrid_reranker_v1
configurations.notes: Generic cross-encoder already gives +23.7% MRR. This was our prod config for 6 months.

configurations.name: hybrid_reranker_v2
configurations.description: Hybrid retrieval + domain fine-tuned cross-encoder (200K pairs)
configurations.notes: Fine-tuning on domain data gave another +5.2% MRR. Training took 4hrs on 4x A10G, totally worth it.

configurations.name: hybrid_reranker_v2_parent_chunks
configurations.description: Full pipeline with parent chunk context expansion
configurations.notes: Parent chunks add context for the reranker to work with. +0.8% MRR for +1.8ms, easy win.
